summary: Completed comprehensive testing of JARVIS AI Assistant. All 17 backend API tests passed. All frontend flows tested successfully including login, dashboard panels, terminal commands, developer mode, and logout.
critical_code_review_comments: Dashboard.js has React warning: headers object in useCallback dependencies should be memoized or moved inside callback
Consider adding error boundaries for better error handling in React components
test_credentials: No credentials needed - biometric login auto-authenticates
seed_data_creation: None required - app uses session-based auth
context_for_next_testing_agent: All core features working: biometric login, terminal commands with Gemini AI, developer mode code generation, system metrics, weather widget, calendar, arc reactor visualization, and logout. Backend uses emergentintegrations library for Gemini AI. Token stored in localStorage as 'jarvis_token'. Protected endpoints require X-JARVIS-TOKEN header.

features_tested.login_flow: PASS - Bio-scan button triggers POST to /api/auth/login, redirects to dashboard
features_tested.dashboard_panels: PASS - Calendar, System Diagnostics, Weather, Status, Terminal, Arc Reactor all visible
features_tested.api_auth_login: PASS - Returns token on biometric login
features_tested.api_command: PASS - Processes commands with Gemini AI
features_tested.api_code_assist: PASS - Generates code with language selection
features_tested.api_weather: PASS - Returns weather data from wttr.in
features_tested.api_status: PASS - Returns system status with LLM info
features_tested.terminal_input: PASS - Sends commands and displays JARVIS responses
features_tested.developer_mode: PASS - Tab switch works, accepts coding prompts
features_tested.logout: PASS - Returns to login screen, clears token
